stories.title: Iris e Andrea, tutto sulla coppia di Temptation Island: le chat (intime) di lui, le bugie e il rapporto con la single Sara
stories.summary: A new season of Temptation Island premieres Wednesday, June 24, on Canale 5. The show follows couples Iris and Andrea as they navigate temptations and lies, with Andrea’s private chats and his relationship with single participant Sara under scrutiny. The series will air in prime time, exploring emotional challenges through hidden cameras and confrontations. Viewers can expect weekly episodes delving into trust and fidelity.

stories.title: Bizkaia mantendrá el peaje de la AP-68 y el viaje ocasional entre Bilbao-Vitoria costará 4,2 euros
stories.summary: Starting November 11, the AP-68 highway in the Basque region will be managed by Interbiak, with occasional trips between Bilbao and Vitoria costing 4.20 euros. From January 1, 2027, users will face spending limits based on usage, with frequent drivers paying up to 35 euros and others up to 53 euros. The new system will save drivers 5 euros per month. The highway will remain toll-free in certain regions, including Aragón, Navarra, La Rioja, and Burgos.

stories.title: ‘Stuart Fails To Save The Universe’: Here Is Why Episodes Of ‘Big Bang Theory’ Spinoff Are So Short
stories.summary: The new HBO Max spinoff Stuart Fails to Save the Universe features 10 episodes, most under 25 minutes long, despite no time constraints. The show follows Stuart Bloom, a comic book store owner, as he navigates fractured realities caused by a malfunctioning quantum machine built by his former Big Bang Theory friends. Each episode ends with the team jumping to a new alternate universe, setting up the next installment. The short runtime reflects the creators' decision to avoid unnecessary padding, even in a streaming format.
stories.trend: stuart fails to save the universe

stories.title: Lazio, Romagnoli convocato in ritiro dopo lo stallo con l'Al-Sadd: giovedì le visite mediche
stories.summary: Lazio defender Alessio Romagnoli has been recalled from a potential transfer to Al-Sadd in Qatar due to a change in the club's budget caused by the ongoing conflict between Israel and Iran. Romagnoli will undergo medical checks and resume training with Lazio on Thursday. He has a year left on his contract with the Italian club. The transfer talks with Al-Sadd are expected to continue despite the setback.

stories.title: Davide Bonolis di nuovo a Formentera con Martina Dotti, la dedica social: "E intanto il tempo passa e tu non passi mai"
stories.summary: Davide Bonolis and Martina Dotti celebrated their one-year anniversary on Formentera, posting photos together on social media. The 22-year-old Bonolis, son of Paolo Bonolis and Sonia Bruganelli, shared the trip with his 24-year-old girlfriend, a popular content creator. The couple, who met at a football match in 2024, hinted at future plans including marriage.

stories.title: 'Lizard Music': Theo Clark Leber Joins Dwayne Johnson In Benny Safdie Pic
stories.summary: Ten-year-old Theo Clark Leber stars as the lead in Lizard Music, Benny Safdie’s live-action/CGI adaptation of Daniel Pinkwater’s 1976 novel for Amazon MGM Studios. He joins Dwayne Johnson, who plays the eccentric Chicken Man in the film. Production is underway with Safdie and Johnson reuniting after their 2025 collaboration The Smashing Machine. Release details remain unannounced.

stories.title: ¿Quiénes eran los cuatro miembros de las Fuerzas Armadas de EE.UU. recientemente muertos en la guerra con Irán?
stories.summary: Four US military personnel were killed in clashes related to Iran, bringing the total number of US military fatalities in the region to 18 since February 28. The four were killed in attacks by Iranian missiles and drones in Jordan and Iraq. US President Donald Trump received their remains with honors at a solemn ceremony at the Dover Air Force Base in Delaware, where he expressed that receiving the remains of fallen military personnel is one of the most difficult tasks as president. The ceremony marks the third time Trump has attended a transfer of remains since the conflict began.
stories.trend: fuerzas armadas de los estados unidos

stories.title: Coronation Street's Katherine Kelly hailed 'breathtaking' in new Eve Myles drama
stories.summary: Katherine Kelly’s performance in The Crow Girl has earned high praise from co-star Eve Myles, who called her "extraordinary" and "breathtaking." The thriller, based on Erik Axl Sund’s novel, follows DCI Jeanette Kilburn (Myles) and psychotherapist Dr Sophia Craven (Kelly) as they hunt a killer. The first series airs on Channel 5, with the second on Paramount+, and viewers have praised the suspenseful drama. The show also stars Dougray Scott.

stories.title: Obama Takes Shot at Vance Over Marriage ‘Hypocrisy’
stories.summary: Former President Barack Obama criticized Vice President JD Vance for perceived hypocrisy in his stance on immigration, pointing out that Vance's wife is a daughter of an immigrant from India. Obama made the comments on a podcast discussing MAGA ideas and the Trump administration's push to end birthright citizenship. The issue affects millions of Americans, including those born to immigrant parents. Obama's comments come as the debate over immigration and citizenship continues.

stories.title: Death of teen in crash as gardaí pursued ruled accidental
stories.summary: A 14-year-old boy died in a car crash five years ago in Killarney, Co Kerry, after being pursued by a garda van with its blue lights flashing. The inquest returned a verdict of accidental death. The driver of the van, Garda Seán O'Sullivan, lost sight of the car as it rounded a bend and sped away. The Garda Síochána Ombudsman Commission cleared Gda O'Sullivan of any responsibility in 2022.

stories.title: Ryanair Spain flight suffers 'technical issue' and met by emergency services
stories.summary: A Ryanair flight from Malaga to Edinburgh declared an emergency mid-air Wednesday, triggering the 7700 squawk code before landing safely at Edinburgh Airport just after 8am. Emergency services met the plane on the tarmac as standard procedure. Ryanair confirmed a minor technical issue and said passengers disembarked normally while engineers conducted a routine inspection. No injuries were reported.

stories.title: “Spéciale Pieds nus et culottés : objectif Drôme” : un épisode spécial non dénué de lourdeur
stories.summary: Nans and Mouts return for a special episode of Pieds Nus & Culottés in the Drôme region on July 22, 2026. The duo reunites with three couples they met during past travels, challenging them to a barefoot, cashless adventure. The special includes Zoé and Louise, their female counterparts, along with Belgian entrepreneurs Peter and Ronald, and Camargue couple Jean-Phi and Laure. The show explores themes of connection and simplicity, revisiting the spirit of the original series.

stories.title: Genetic genealogy, DNA from trash help solve Arlington killing 14 years after woman found stabbed 100 times, police say
stories.summary: Arlington police have charged Mayra Velasquez, 42, with capital murder in the 2012 killing of Irasema Chavez, 32, who was found stabbed nearly 100 times in her apartment on January 20, 2012. Velasquez is being held in the Tarrant County Jail without bond. The case was reopened and solved through new forensic analysis and DNA evidence from trash. A real estate agent, Velasquez's license remains active.

stories.title: ‘Spider-Man: Brand New Day’ Final Trailer Unveils Action-Packed New Footage of Mark Ruffalo’s Hulk Battling the Web-Slinger
stories.summary: A new trailer for Spider-Man: Brand New Day has been released, featuring action-packed footage of Mark Ruffalo's Hulk battling Tom Holland's Spider-Man in New York City. The film, a sequel to the prior trilogy, includes appearances by Sadie Sink, Florence Pugh, and Zabryna Guevara. The story follows Peter Parker's journey to find MJ, with whom he shares a tearful farewell. The final trailer hints at a battle between Spider-Man and the Hand, with a mysterious cloaked figure emerging as a new threat.

stories.title: Man charged over incident where car driven into group
stories.summary: A 29-year-old man has been charged with dangerous driving after a car was driven into a group of people in Skerries, resulting in a court appearance and bail being granted. Sean McGlone, from Drogheda, was arrested at his home and charged at Balbriggan Garda Station. He was released on a €5,000 bond with a €1,000 lodgement, subject to strict conditions including signing on three times a week and avoiding Skerries. McGlone is due to appear in court again.

stories.title: Watch Maidenhead friendly on Wolves TV | Men's First-Team | News
stories.summary: Wolverhampton Wanderers beat Maidenhead United 1-0 in their opening pre-season friendly at York Road on Wednesday 22 July. The only goal came in the first half, with Wolves dominating possession and chances despite Maidenhead’s late pressure. The match streamed live on Wolves TV, with free access for account holders. Next up, Wolves face Real Sociedad in their second pre-season fixture on Saturday 25 July.

stories.title: CROSS ADMITS MARRIAGE SPLIT BROUGHT “STRUCTURE” TO HIS LIFE
stories.summary: Rob Cross admitted his marriage split helped focus his career after qualifying for the World Matchplay second round. The 2021 champion beat Danny Noppert 10-3 in Blackpool on Monday night. Cross said his manager’s pep talk and his personal drive pushed him through the opening stage. He faces the next opponent on Tuesday.

stories.title: World Matchplay 2026: Cameron Menzies receives medical care after being taken ill on stage
stories.summary: Cameron Menzies, a Scotland darts player, was taken ill during his World Matchplay second-round match in Blackpool on Wednesday, experiencing high blood pressure that caused him to feel dizzy and faint. He was cleared by medics backstage but unable to return to the stage, forfeiting the match. Menzies was deemed well enough to return to his accommodation. The incident occurred while he was leading 2-1 against Ross Smith.

stories.title: Le voyage vire au cauchemar : les médecins lui découvrent "une tumeur importante au niveau du cerveau" alors qu’il est à l’autre bout du monde
stories.summary: A 30-year-old French engineer from Lyon vanished in Peru on July 10 while traveling alone near Puerto Malabrigo. After a nine-day search, he was found alive but disoriented in Trujillo, about 70 km away. Doctors later diagnosed a significant brain tumor. His parents, who flew to Peru, are now arranging urgent medical care for him.

stories.title: Google earnings live updates: Alphabet's AI outlook, cloud growth in focus
stories.summary: Google's earnings report highlights a record 1.7 billion unique viewers globally watching World Cup-related videos on YouTube. The company's cloud growth and AI outlook are also in focus. Alphabet's other bets category, including Waymo and Verily, saw a 2.4% year-over-year increase. Google's YouTube advertising revenue rose 13% in the second quarter.

stories.title: Did Princess Diana's brother criticize Queen Camilla in royal gala speech?
stories.summary: Charles Spencer referenced Princess Diana’s personal writings during a July 2026 royal gala speech, transferring her diaries to a trust overseen by her sons. The move contradicted the palace’s push for unity tied to Queen Camilla. Spencer’s 1997 eulogy for Diana had previously sparked controversy. The event reignited long-standing tensions within the royal family.

stories.title: En direct Football. Suivez Glasgow Rangers - ASSE : Breum redonne le sourire aux Verts
stories.summary: Glasgow Rangers defeated ASSE 2-1 in a match at Ibrox Stadium in Glasgow on July 22, 2026. The Scots scored two penalties, with Shankland and Aasgaard netting in the 5th and 85th minutes respectively. The loss for ASSE comes despite a strong overall performance from the team. The match marked a comeback for ASSE after being down, but ultimately fell short.

stories.title: Roggero resta in carcere, no del tribunale al differimento della pena
stories.summary: Mario Roggero, a 14 years and 9 months in prison for killing two and injuring a third in a 2021 self-defense incident, has had his request for a reduced sentence denied by the Torino Ufficio di Sorveglianza. The decision will now be reviewed by the Torino Tribunale di Sorveglianza and the Procura generale della Corte d'Appello. Roggero's lawyers will continue to pursue a solution, possibly through a grant of house arrest or a pardon.

stories.title: ¿'Quo vadis', Rodri? El deseo del Balón de Oro español y la tesitura del Real Madrid
stories.summary: Spanish midfielder Rodri, a potential Ballon d'Or winner, has confirmed he will not decide his future until after the World Cup. He has one year left on his contract with Manchester City and is reportedly considering a move to Real Madrid, his hometown club, where he was born and raised in Villanueva de la Cañada. Rodri's desire is to return to Spain, where he won the World Cup with the national team. His next move will be closely watched, especially after Manchester City presented him with a contract renewal offer.

stories.title: Tom Bischof machte einst den Karten-Jubel: FC Bayern-Star enthüllt Uno-Deal
stories.summary: FC Bayern star Tom Bischof has signed a new sponsorship deal with the popular card game Uno. The 21-year-old footballer will wear the number 8 on his jersey, a nod to the game's significance in his life. Bischof, an avid Uno player, made headlines earlier this year for his on-field Uno celebration. He will now promote the game through his social media channels.

stories.title: Inter Miami under investigation after signing Casemiro
stories.summary: Major League Soccer is investigating Inter Miami for alleged tampering after signing Brazil midfielder Casemiro on a deal through 2027 with a 2029 option. The league confirmed Wednesday it is reviewing the move, as the LA Galaxy held Casemiro’s discovery rights. Miami claims it acquired those rights, but MLS has not yet released settlement terms. The investigation remains ongoing, with no further comments expected until its conclusion.

stories.title: Menzies taken ill on stage at World Matchplay
stories.summary: Scotland’s Cameron Menzies forfeited his World Matchplay second-round match in Blackpool on Wednesday after collapsing on stage with high blood pressure. Medics cleared him but he couldn’t continue, handing Ross Smith a walkover. Menzies was escorted off and later returned to his accommodation. Meanwhile, world number three Gian van Veen advanced to the quarter-finals with a 14-12 win over Wessel Nijman.

stories.title: Brain injury cases should be thrown out, High Court told
stories.summary: A High Court hearing on Wednesday heard arguments over whether to dismiss 510 brain injury claims against rugby league and union bodies for failing to comply with court orders. Lawyers for World Rugby and the RFU called dismissal a last resort due to ongoing non-compliance, while players’ representatives argued the breaches were minor and claims should proceed. The case centers on whether procedural failures justify throwing out claims in a litigation involving former players. A ruling is expected in coming weeks.

stories.title: ‘Stuart Saves the Universe’ Producers on Pulling Off Such a Crazy ‘Big Bang Theory’ Spinoff After ‘No One Really Liked the Idea’
stories.summary: Chuck Lorre pitched a "Big Bang Theory" spinoff featuring Stuart Bloom navigating a portal to other dimensions, but network execs rejected the unconventional idea despite the original show's success. The rejected concept would have starred Kevin Sussman in an action-comedy-sci-fi mashup. Lorre aimed for something entirely different from typical sitcom spinoffs. The pitch never moved forward after initial pushback.

stories.title: Tesla’s revenues are bouncing back, but profits are still weak
stories.summary: Tesla reported a 26% revenue jump to $28.2 billion in Q2 2026, selling 480,126 vehicles—up 25% from last year. Net income rose just 5% to $1.11 billion despite the sales surge. The company trimmed inventory but profits lagged Wall Street’s $26.4 billion revenue forecast. Next, Tesla faces pressure to boost margins amid shifting EV demand.

stories.title: Nexstar Accused of Violating Injunction by Appointing Tegna Board
stories.summary: A coalition of 13 attorneys general and DirecTV accused Nexstar of violating a court injunction by appointing its executives to Tegna’s board. The April order required both companies to remain independent pending an antitrust trial. Nexstar denied wrongdoing, stating it has complied with the hold-separate order. The plaintiffs asked the judge to clarify the injunction’s restrictions.

stories.title: TV-Tipp: "Helix"
stories.summary: A thriller novel "Helix" by Marc Elsberg, published in 2016, explores the consequences of genetic engineering. The story spans the globe, involving the murder of the US Secretary of State and the kidnapping of the US President. The novel's central theme revolves around the possibility of creating genetically tailored children through targeted DNA interventions. As the plot unfolds, the characters' actions raise questions about the ethics of playing God with human genetics.

stories.title: Conservatives Parrot A Victimhood Script Written By Barack Obama
stories.summary: Former US President Donald Trump's 2024 presidential election win is attributed to TikTok by Wall Street Journal columnist Allysia Finley, who claims the platform fuels socialism and opposition to the Iran war, yet may have helped Trump due to algorithm tweaks. The alleged changes could have been made to aid Trump in suspending TikTok's ban. Finley's theory is based on the seeming contradiction between TikTok's supposed anti-establishment views and Trump's election victory. The outcome of this theory remains unverified.

stories.title: ‘Pompeii: Out of Time with Tom Hiddleston’: A Rare Doc That Could Actually Attract a Young Audience
stories.summary: Tom Hiddleston stars in a three-part Nat Geo docuseries, Pompeii: Out of Time with Tom Hiddleston, blending time-travel drama with expert insights to explore Pompeii’s final hours after Mount Vesuvius erupted in A.D. 79. The series, executive produced with Kevin R. Wright, follows Hiddleston as a "Time Detective" examining ancient records and artifacts to uncover the resilience of Pompeii’s inhabitants. Premiering in 2024, it merges scripted storytelling with interviews from archaeologists and geologists. The hybrid format aims to engage younger audiences by reimagining historical disaster through modern media consumption habits.

stories.title: US to announce deal allowing Saudi Arabia a nuclear programme
stories.summary: The U.S. and Saudi Arabia are set to announce a civilian nuclear deal on Wednesday, allowing the kingdom to develop a nuclear program with U.S. company involvement. The agreement could generate billions for American firms, which may build and operate enrichment facilities enabling Saudi Arabia to produce its own nuclear fuel. While framed as peaceful, critics warn the deal risks enabling weapons-grade uranium production. Congress must approve the pact before finalization.

stories.title: HSV-Star deutlich: "Von solchen Aussagen halte ich gar nichts"
stories.summary: Hamburger SV goalkeeper Daniel Heuer Fernandes has expressed his commitment to the team, stating he will continue to perform at his best to help the team improve. The 33-year-old goalkeeper has been with the HSV for seven years and is currently in a training camp in Ötztal, Austria, preparing for the upcoming season. The team is currently ranked in a position that could lead to relegation. Fernandes will need to maintain his performance to help the team avoid relegation.

stories.title: AD-columnist doet voorspelling en wijst naar PSV: 'Maar het ligt heel dicht bij elkaar'
stories.summary: PSV is expected to win its fourth consecutive Eredivisie title in the 2026/2027 season, having secured the championship with a record 19-point lead over Feyenoord last season. The team's chances are expected to be challenged by a more competitive title race. PSV's success will be closely watched, particularly with Feyenoord's Sem Steijn, the son of former Sparta Rotterdam coach Maurice Steijn, looking to make a strong comeback after a setback.

stories.title: Tv-programma De Avondetappe dinsdagavond opnieuw te zien vanuit Metaal Kathedraal in Utrecht
stories.summary: The TV program De Avondetappe will be broadcast live from the Metaal Kathedraal in Utrecht on Tuesday evening, following a successful outing on Monday with 1.1 million viewers. The show, hosted by Dione de Graaff, will take place in the industrial area of the cultural center. This marks the first regular broadcast since the World Cup, resuming its usual 9:30 PM time slot. The program will continue to discuss the Tour de France.

stories.title: Price-hiked iPads are a little cheaper right now
stories.summary: A 13-inch iPad Pro M5 with 512GB storage is now $1,549.99 at Amazon, down from $1,699, while Best Buy offers a $100 discount. The 11-inch iPad Air M3 with 1TB storage is also discounted to $749, a $500 reduction from its original price. Both deals come as Apple raised prices on multiple products last month. Shoppers can expect these discounts to vary by retailer and availability.

stories.title: Tesla set to report second-quarter results after the bell
stories.summary: Tesla reported weaker-than-expected second-quarter earnings after the bell Wednesday, with adjusted earnings per share at 33 cents versus the 51-cent estimate. Revenue reached $28.24 billion, beating forecasts of $25.71 billion, but net income fell 5% to $1.11 billion. The stock slid nearly 3% in extended trading as Tesla’s shares remain down 17% for the year amid broader market declines. The company’s automotive segment drove most of the growth, generating $20.52 billion in revenue.

stories.title: Coveted U.S. Nuclear Deal Is a Coup for Saudi Crown Prince
stories.summary: Saudi Arabia had for years sought the controversial deal to obtain American nuclear technology. It is expected to be sealed on Wednesday, deepening U.S.-Saudi ties for decades to come.

stories.title: U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal Stokes Israeli Concerns of Mideast Arms Race
stories.summary: An Israeli minister sought to downplay the agreement, while critics said it could spur other regional countries to pursue a nuclear program, and potentially the creation of a bomb.

stories.title: Canadian Wildfire Evacuees Outraged Over Lacking Support and Communication
stories.summary: Residents of northwestern Ontario say their warnings about fires they spotted were dismissed and official information as the flames spread often didn’t come at all.

stories.title: Alphabet Quadruples Profit to Nearly $120 Billion, Fueled by A.I. Investments
stories.summary: Google’s parent company, which has spent heavily on artificial intelligence, also showed growth across its businesses, particularly in cloud computing.

stories.title: House Passes Defense Bill Amid Iran War Divide
stories.summary: Republicans pushed through the $1.15 trillion bill over opposition from Democrats, who said backing it was akin to embracing the war.

stories.title: Police formally investigate woman after Brit stabbed to death in French village
stories.summary: A woman in France has been formally charged in the 2024 stabbing death of British-South African mother Karen Carter, 65, in the Dordogne village of Trémolat. The 70-year-old suspect, who was briefly held in 2025 before release, now faces potential trial after prosecutors found sufficient evidence. Carter, known locally for managing holiday rentals, was found near her car with fatal wounds. Authorities continue gathering details ahead of the next legal steps.

stories.title: Trump’s 50% Tariffs on Canada: What to Know, and What’s Next
stories.summary: The president said punishing 50 percent tariffs would begin by Aug. 19, a significant escalation of the trade war between Canada and the U.S. that he started over a year ago.

stories.title: Speaking for Fallen Soldiers, Trump Says They Supported the Iran War
stories.summary: President Trump attended a dignified transfer for the soldiers as Americans were growing increasingly critical of the war.

stories.title: Trump threatens to target Iran's bridges and power plants if Hormuz attacks persist
stories.summary: President Donald Trump warned on Truth Social that the U.S. would strike Iranian bridges or power plants, including near Tehran, if attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz continue. His threat follows 11 consecutive nights of U.S. strikes on Iran, with explosions reported in multiple cities, including near Iran’s only nuclear power plant in Bushehr. Iran has vowed retaliation after Trump said the U.S. would soon target an underground nuclear site near Natanz. Kuwait, Bahrain, and Jordan reported intercepting Iranian drones in recent days.

stories.title: NASA to Showcase Agency’s Newest Wind Tunnel in Virginia
stories.summary: NASA will unveil its first new wind tunnel in over 40 years at the Langley Research Center in Hampton, Virginia, on July 31. The Flight Dynamics Research Facility ribbon-cutting includes NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman and center director Dr. Trina Dyal. The event is restricted to U.S. media with valid credentials. This upgrade supports next-generation aerospace research and testing.

stories.title: What Were the Lotus-Eaters Eating?
stories.summary: In Homer’s epic, Odysseus’s men eat something so powerful that they forget about home. Which plants could have produced such a soporific, narcotic and amnesiac effect?

stories.title: Donald Trump Jr.’s Investment Firm Posts Staggering Returns of 200%
stories.summary: The fledgling firm, run by the president’s oldest son and his partner Omeed Malik, has made investments in A.I. and defense technology that have tripled in value in just months.

stories.title: Global Oil Price Jumps as War Threats Intensify
stories.summary: But stock investors shrug off Iran developments as they await crucial earnings reports from tech firms including Alphabet and Tesla.

stories.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
stories.summary: Ultra-processed foods, particularly those high in sugar and refined carbs, are the primary drivers of obesity in Britain, according to a cross-party report. MPs urge targeting these foods to reduce obesity rates, highlighting flaws in current food policy that focuses on fat, salt, and sugar equally. The report calls for stricter accountability for supermarkets and manufacturers. Next, the government is expected to review nutritional guidelines to prioritize sugar reduction.

stories.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
stories.summary: The administration canceled federal grants to organizations across the country, accusing them of encouraging premarital sex. New criteria say programs should stress abstinence.

offbeat.title: Iris e Andrea, tutto sulla coppia di Temptation Island: le chat (intime) di lui, le bugie e il rapporto con la single Sara
offbeat.summary: A new season of Temptation Island premieres Wednesday, June 24, on Canale 5. The show follows couples Iris and Andrea as they navigate temptations and lies, with Andrea’s private chats and his relationship with single participant Sara under scrutiny. The series will air in prime time, exploring emotional challenges through hidden cameras and confrontations. Viewers can expect weekly episodes delving into trust and fidelity.

offbeat.summary: A Ryanair flight from Malaga to Edinburgh declared an emergency mid-air Wednesday, triggering the 7700 squawk code before landing safely at Edinburgh Airport just after 8am. Emergency services met the plane on the tarmac as standard procedure. Ryanair confirmed a minor technical issue and said passengers disembarked normally while engineers conducted a routine inspection. No injuries were reported.

offbeat.title: “Spéciale Pieds nus et culottés : objectif Drôme” : un épisode spécial non dénué de lourdeur
offbeat.summary: Nans and Mouts return for a special episode of Pieds Nus & Culottés in the Drôme region on July 22, 2026. The duo reunites with three couples they met during past travels, challenging them to a barefoot, cashless adventure. The special includes Zoé and Louise, their female counterparts, along with Belgian entrepreneurs Peter and Ronald, and Camargue couple Jean-Phi and Laure. The show explores themes of connection and simplicity, revisiting the spirit of the original series.

offbeat.title: Genetic genealogy, DNA from trash help solve Arlington killing 14 years after woman found stabbed 100 times, police say
offbeat.summary: Arlington police have charged Mayra Velasquez, 42, with capital murder in the 2012 killing of Irasema Chavez, 32, who was found stabbed nearly 100 times in her apartment on January 20, 2012. Velasquez is being held in the Tarrant County Jail without bond. The case was reopened and solved through new forensic analysis and DNA evidence from trash. A real estate agent, Velasquez's license remains active.

offbeat.title: ‘Spider-Man: Brand New Day’ Final Trailer Unveils Action-Packed New Footage of Mark Ruffalo’s Hulk Battling the Web-Slinger
offbeat.summary: A new trailer for Spider-Man: Brand New Day has been released, featuring action-packed footage of Mark Ruffalo's Hulk battling Tom Holland's Spider-Man in New York City. The film, a sequel to the prior trilogy, includes appearances by Sadie Sink, Florence Pugh, and Zabryna Guevara. The story follows Peter Parker's journey to find MJ, with whom he shares a tearful farewell. The final trailer hints at a battle between Spider-Man and the Hand, with a mysterious cloaked figure emerging as a new threat.

offbeat.title: Man charged over incident where car driven into group
offbeat.summary: A 29-year-old man has been charged with dangerous driving after a car was driven into a group of people in Skerries, resulting in a court appearance and bail being granted. Sean McGlone, from Drogheda, was arrested at his home and charged at Balbriggan Garda Station. He was released on a €5,000 bond with a €1,000 lodgement, subject to strict conditions including signing on three times a week and avoiding Skerries. McGlone is due to appear in court again.

offbeat.title: Watch Maidenhead friendly on Wolves TV | Men's First-Team | News
offbeat.summary: Wolverhampton Wanderers beat Maidenhead United 1-0 in their opening pre-season friendly at York Road on Wednesday 22 July. The only goal came in the first half, with Wolves dominating possession and chances despite Maidenhead’s late pressure. The match streamed live on Wolves TV, with free access for account holders. Next up, Wolves face Real Sociedad in their second pre-season fixture on Saturday 25 July.

offbeat.title: CROSS ADMITS MARRIAGE SPLIT BROUGHT “STRUCTURE” TO HIS LIFE
offbeat.summary: Rob Cross admitted his marriage split helped focus his career after qualifying for the World Matchplay second round. The 2021 champion beat Danny Noppert 10-3 in Blackpool on Monday night. Cross said his manager’s pep talk and his personal drive pushed him through the opening stage. He faces the next opponent on Tuesday.

offbeat.title: World Matchplay 2026: Cameron Menzies receives medical care after being taken ill on stage
offbeat.summary: Cameron Menzies, a Scotland darts player, was taken ill during his World Matchplay second-round match in Blackpool on Wednesday, experiencing high blood pressure that caused him to feel dizzy and faint. He was cleared by medics backstage but unable to return to the stage, forfeiting the match. Menzies was deemed well enough to return to his accommodation. The incident occurred while he was leading 2-1 against Ross Smith.

offbeat.title: Le voyage vire au cauchemar : les médecins lui découvrent "une tumeur importante au niveau du cerveau" alors qu’il est à l’autre bout du monde
offbeat.summary: A 30-year-old French engineer from Lyon vanished in Peru on July 10 while traveling alone near Puerto Malabrigo. After a nine-day search, he was found alive but disoriented in Trujillo, about 70 km away. Doctors later diagnosed a significant brain tumor. His parents, who flew to Peru, are now arranging urgent medical care for him.

offbeat.title: Google earnings live updates: Alphabet's AI outlook, cloud growth in focus
offbeat.summary: Google's earnings report highlights a record 1.7 billion unique viewers globally watching World Cup-related videos on YouTube. The company's cloud growth and AI outlook are also in focus. Alphabet's other bets category, including Waymo and Verily, saw a 2.4% year-over-year increase. Google's YouTube advertising revenue rose 13% in the second quarter.

offbeat.title: Did Princess Diana's brother criticize Queen Camilla in royal gala speech?
offbeat.summary: Charles Spencer referenced Princess Diana’s personal writings during a July 2026 royal gala speech, transferring her diaries to a trust overseen by her sons. The move contradicted the palace’s push for unity tied to Queen Camilla. Spencer’s 1997 eulogy for Diana had previously sparked controversy. The event reignited long-standing tensions within the royal family.

offbeat.title: En direct Football. Suivez Glasgow Rangers - ASSE : Breum redonne le sourire aux Verts
offbeat.summary: Glasgow Rangers defeated ASSE 2-1 in a match at Ibrox Stadium in Glasgow on July 22, 2026. The Scots scored two penalties, with Shankland and Aasgaard netting in the 5th and 85th minutes respectively. The loss for ASSE comes despite a strong overall performance from the team. The match marked a comeback for ASSE after being down, but ultimately fell short.

offbeat.title: Roggero resta in carcere, no del tribunale al differimento della pena
offbeat.summary: Mario Roggero, a 14 years and 9 months in prison for killing two and injuring a third in a 2021 self-defense incident, has had his request for a reduced sentence denied by the Torino Ufficio di Sorveglianza. The decision will now be reviewed by the Torino Tribunale di Sorveglianza and the Procura generale della Corte d'Appello. Roggero's lawyers will continue to pursue a solution, possibly through a grant of house arrest or a pardon.

offbeat.title: ¿'Quo vadis', Rodri? El deseo del Balón de Oro español y la tesitura del Real Madrid
offbeat.summary: Spanish midfielder Rodri, a potential Ballon d'Or winner, has confirmed he will not decide his future until after the World Cup. He has one year left on his contract with Manchester City and is reportedly considering a move to Real Madrid, his hometown club, where he was born and raised in Villanueva de la Cañada. Rodri's desire is to return to Spain, where he won the World Cup with the national team. His next move will be closely watched, especially after Manchester City presented him with a contract renewal offer.

offbeat.title: Tom Bischof machte einst den Karten-Jubel: FC Bayern-Star enthüllt Uno-Deal
offbeat.summary: FC Bayern star Tom Bischof has signed a new sponsorship deal with the popular card game Uno. The 21-year-old footballer will wear the number 8 on his jersey, a nod to the game's significance in his life. Bischof, an avid Uno player, made headlines earlier this year for his on-field Uno celebration. He will now promote the game through his social media channels.

offbeat.title: TV-Tipp: "Helix"
offbeat.summary: A thriller novel "Helix" by Marc Elsberg, published in 2016, explores the consequences of genetic engineering. The story spans the globe, involving the murder of the US Secretary of State and the kidnapping of the US President. The novel's central theme revolves around the possibility of creating genetically tailored children through targeted DNA interventions. As the plot unfolds, the characters' actions raise questions about the ethics of playing God with human genetics.

offbeat.title: Conservatives Parrot A Victimhood Script Written By Barack Obama
offbeat.summary: Former US President Donald Trump's 2024 presidential election win is attributed to TikTok by Wall Street Journal columnist Allysia Finley, who claims the platform fuels socialism and opposition to the Iran war, yet may have helped Trump due to algorithm tweaks. The alleged changes could have been made to aid Trump in suspending TikTok's ban. Finley's theory is based on the seeming contradiction between TikTok's supposed anti-establishment views and Trump's election victory. The outcome of this theory remains unverified.

offbeat.title: US to announce deal allowing Saudi Arabia a nuclear programme
offbeat.summary: The U.S. and Saudi Arabia are set to announce a civilian nuclear deal on Wednesday, allowing the kingdom to develop a nuclear program with U.S. company involvement. The agreement could generate billions for American firms, which may build and operate enrichment facilities enabling Saudi Arabia to produce its own nuclear fuel. While framed as peaceful, critics warn the deal risks enabling weapons-grade uranium production. Congress must approve the pact before finalization.

offbeat.title: HSV-Star deutlich: "Von solchen Aussagen halte ich gar nichts"
offbeat.summary: Hamburger SV goalkeeper Daniel Heuer Fernandes has expressed his commitment to the team, stating he will continue to perform at his best to help the team improve. The 33-year-old goalkeeper has been with the HSV for seven years and is currently in a training camp in Ötztal, Austria, preparing for the upcoming season. The team is currently ranked in a position that could lead to relegation. Fernandes will need to maintain his performance to help the team avoid relegation.

offbeat.title: AD-columnist doet voorspelling en wijst naar PSV: 'Maar het ligt heel dicht bij elkaar'
offbeat.summary: PSV is expected to win its fourth consecutive Eredivisie title in the 2026/2027 season, having secured the championship with a record 19-point lead over Feyenoord last season. The team's chances are expected to be challenged by a more competitive title race. PSV's success will be closely watched, particularly with Feyenoord's Sem Steijn, the son of former Sparta Rotterdam coach Maurice Steijn, looking to make a strong comeback after a setback.

offbeat.title: Tv-programma De Avondetappe dinsdagavond opnieuw te zien vanuit Metaal Kathedraal in Utrecht
offbeat.summary: The TV program De Avondetappe will be broadcast live from the Metaal Kathedraal in Utrecht on Tuesday evening, following a successful outing on Monday with 1.1 million viewers. The show, hosted by Dione de Graaff, will take place in the industrial area of the cultural center. This marks the first regular broadcast since the World Cup, resuming its usual 9:30 PM time slot. The program will continue to discuss the Tour de France.

offbeat.title: Tesla set to report second-quarter results after the bell
offbeat.summary: Tesla reported weaker-than-expected second-quarter earnings after the bell Wednesday, with adjusted earnings per share at 33 cents versus the 51-cent estimate. Revenue reached $28.24 billion, beating forecasts of $25.71 billion, but net income fell 5% to $1.11 billion. The stock slid nearly 3% in extended trading as Tesla’s shares remain down 17% for the year amid broader market declines. The company’s automotive segment drove most of the growth, generating $20.52 billion in revenue.

offbeat.title: Police formally investigate woman after Brit stabbed to death in French village
offbeat.summary: A woman in France has been formally charged in the 2024 stabbing death of British-South African mother Karen Carter, 65, in the Dordogne village of Trémolat. The 70-year-old suspect, who was briefly held in 2025 before release, now faces potential trial after prosecutors found sufficient evidence. Carter, known locally for managing holiday rentals, was found near her car with fatal wounds. Authorities continue gathering details ahead of the next legal steps.

offbeat.title: Trump threatens to target Iran's bridges and power plants if Hormuz attacks persist
offbeat.summary: President Donald Trump warned on Truth Social that the U.S. would strike Iranian bridges or power plants, including near Tehran, if attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz continue. His threat follows 11 consecutive nights of U.S. strikes on Iran, with explosions reported in multiple cities, including near Iran’s only nuclear power plant in Bushehr. Iran has vowed retaliation after Trump said the U.S. would soon target an underground nuclear site near Natanz. Kuwait, Bahrain, and Jordan reported intercepting Iranian drones in recent days.

sports.title: Inter Miami under investigation after signing Casemiro
sports.summary: Major League Soccer is investigating Inter Miami for alleged tampering after signing Brazil midfielder Casemiro on a deal through 2027 with a 2029 option. The league confirmed Wednesday it is reviewing the move, as the LA Galaxy held Casemiro’s discovery rights. Miami claims it acquired those rights, but MLS has not yet released settlement terms. The investigation remains ongoing, with no further comments expected until its conclusion.

sports.title: Menzies taken ill on stage at World Matchplay
sports.summary: Scotland’s Cameron Menzies forfeited his World Matchplay second-round match in Blackpool on Wednesday after collapsing on stage with high blood pressure. Medics cleared him but he couldn’t continue, handing Ross Smith a walkover. Menzies was escorted off and later returned to his accommodation. Meanwhile, world number three Gian van Veen advanced to the quarter-finals with a 14-12 win over Wessel Nijman.

sports.title: Brain injury cases should be thrown out, High Court told
sports.summary: A High Court hearing on Wednesday heard arguments over whether to dismiss 510 brain injury claims against rugby league and union bodies for failing to comply with court orders. Lawyers for World Rugby and the RFU called dismissal a last resort due to ongoing non-compliance, while players’ representatives argued the breaches were minor and claims should proceed. The case centers on whether procedural failures justify throwing out claims in a litigation involving former players. A ruling is expected in coming weeks.

entertainment.title: ‘Stuart Saves the Universe’ Producers on Pulling Off Such a Crazy ‘Big Bang Theory’ Spinoff After ‘No One Really Liked the Idea’
entertainment.summary: Chuck Lorre pitched a "Big Bang Theory" spinoff featuring Stuart Bloom navigating a portal to other dimensions, but network execs rejected the unconventional idea despite the original show's success. The rejected concept would have starred Kevin Sussman in an action-comedy-sci-fi mashup. Lorre aimed for something entirely different from typical sitcom spinoffs. The pitch never moved forward after initial pushback.

entertainment.title: Nexstar Accused of Violating Injunction by Appointing Tegna Board
entertainment.summary: A coalition of 13 attorneys general and DirecTV accused Nexstar of violating a court injunction by appointing its executives to Tegna’s board. The April order required both companies to remain independent pending an antitrust trial. Nexstar denied wrongdoing, stating it has complied with the hold-separate order. The plaintiffs asked the judge to clarify the injunction’s restrictions.

entertainment.title: ‘Pompeii: Out of Time with Tom Hiddleston’: A Rare Doc That Could Actually Attract a Young Audience
entertainment.summary: Tom Hiddleston stars in a three-part Nat Geo docuseries, Pompeii: Out of Time with Tom Hiddleston, blending time-travel drama with expert insights to explore Pompeii’s final hours after Mount Vesuvius erupted in A.D. 79. The series, executive produced with Kevin R. Wright, follows Hiddleston as a "Time Detective" examining ancient records and artifacts to uncover the resilience of Pompeii’s inhabitants. Premiering in 2024, it merges scripted storytelling with interviews from archaeologists and geologists. The hybrid format aims to engage younger audiences by reimagining historical disaster through modern media consumption habits.

tech.title: Tesla’s revenues are bouncing back, but profits are still weak
tech.summary: Tesla reported a 26% revenue jump to $28.2 billion in Q2 2026, selling 480,126 vehicles—up 25% from last year. Net income rose just 5% to $1.11 billion despite the sales surge. The company trimmed inventory but profits lagged Wall Street’s $26.4 billion revenue forecast. Next, Tesla faces pressure to boost margins amid shifting EV demand.

tech.title: Price-hiked iPads are a little cheaper right now
tech.summary: A 13-inch iPad Pro M5 with 512GB storage is now $1,549.99 at Amazon, down from $1,699, while Best Buy offers a $100 discount. The 11-inch iPad Air M3 with 1TB storage is also discounted to $749, a $500 reduction from its original price. Both deals come as Apple raised prices on multiple products last month. Shoppers can expect these discounts to vary by retailer and availability.

tech.title: Alphabet Quadruples Profit to Nearly $120 Billion, Fueled by A.I. Investments
tech.summary: Google’s parent company, which has spent heavily on artificial intelligence, also showed growth across its businesses, particularly in cloud computing.

breaking.title: Coveted U.S. Nuclear Deal Is a Coup for Saudi Crown Prince
breaking.summary: Saudi Arabia had for years sought the controversial deal to obtain American nuclear technology. It is expected to be sealed on Wednesday, deepening U.S.-Saudi ties for decades to come.

breaking.title: U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal Stokes Israeli Concerns of Mideast Arms Race
breaking.summary: An Israeli minister sought to downplay the agreement, while critics said it could spur other regional countries to pursue a nuclear program, and potentially the creation of a bomb.

breaking.title: Canadian Wildfire Evacuees Outraged Over Lacking Support and Communication
breaking.summary: Residents of northwestern Ontario say their warnings about fires they spotted were dismissed and official information as the flames spread often didn’t come at all.

politics.title: House Passes Defense Bill Amid Iran War Divide
politics.summary: Republicans pushed through the $1.15 trillion bill over opposition from Democrats, who said backing it was akin to embracing the war.

politics.title: Trump’s 50% Tariffs on Canada: What to Know, and What’s Next
politics.summary: The president said punishing 50 percent tariffs would begin by Aug. 19, a significant escalation of the trade war between Canada and the U.S. that he started over a year ago.

politics.title: Speaking for Fallen Soldiers, Trump Says They Supported the Iran War
politics.summary: President Trump attended a dignified transfer for the soldiers as Americans were growing increasingly critical of the war.

facts.title: NASA to Showcase Agency’s Newest Wind Tunnel in Virginia
facts.summary: NASA will unveil its first new wind tunnel in over 40 years at the Langley Research Center in Hampton, Virginia, on July 31. The Flight Dynamics Research Facility ribbon-cutting includes NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman and center director Dr. Trina Dyal. The event is restricted to U.S. media with valid credentials. This upgrade supports next-generation aerospace research and testing.

facts.title: What Were the Lotus-Eaters Eating?
facts.summary: In Homer’s epic, Odysseus’s men eat something so powerful that they forget about home. Which plants could have produced such a soporific, narcotic and amnesiac effect?

crisis.title: Donald Trump Jr.’s Investment Firm Posts Staggering Returns of 200%
crisis.summary: The fledgling firm, run by the president’s oldest son and his partner Omeed Malik, has made investments in A.I. and defense technology that have tripled in value in just months.

crisis.title: Global Oil Price Jumps as War Threats Intensify
crisis.summary: But stock investors shrug off Iran developments as they await crucial earnings reports from tech firms including Alphabet and Tesla.

health.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
health.summary: Ultra-processed foods, particularly those high in sugar and refined carbs, are the primary drivers of obesity in Britain, according to a cross-party report. MPs urge targeting these foods to reduce obesity rates, highlighting flaws in current food policy that focuses on fat, salt, and sugar equally. The report calls for stricter accountability for supermarkets and manufacturers. Next, the government is expected to review nutritional guidelines to prioritize sugar reduction.

health.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
health.summary: The administration canceled federal grants to organizations across the country, accusing them of encouraging premarital sex. New criteria say programs should stress abstinence.
